title: Clone Box Pokémon
description:
# Clone Box Pokémon

Once executed, this code allows you to clone pokémon in the PC by picking them up and dropping them in another slot. This should be done using the "Move Pokémon" option.

## Usage notes

While you are picking up and dropping down pokémon, slots containing cloned pokémon will appear as empty within the PC menu. Exiting and reopening the PC menu will correctly display them.

Only pokémon within the PC will be cloned, party pokémon are unaffected. Until the game is reset, you will not be able to release pokémon.

When using the "Withdraw Pokémon" option, trying to withdraw the same pokémon twice without exiting back out to the PC menu will cause the game to softlock.

## Technical details

The function that's responsible for removing pokémon in the PC get passed along an argument that indicates which box number the to-be-removed pokémon is located in. This value can either have a value corresponding to the actual box ID, or it can have a value of 0xFFFFFFFF (-1 if interpreted as a signed integer) which indicates that the pokémon is to be removed from the current active box.

Because of this, the function first checks if the box ID it received corresponds to -1. If it is, it will overwrite this value with the current box ID. The game then checks whether the pokémon we're trying to move is in a valid slot and a valid box number. If both are valid, then the game removes the pokémon from that specific slot.

This code works by breaking the first check. By sabotaging this check the box ID value of 0xFFFFFFFF is never overwritten, causing the game to think we're trying to remove a pokémon from an invalid box. This causes the game to completely skip removing the pokémon altogether.
author: RETIRE
